headline: Cyril Imhof frames rising frontier AI costs as the driver pushing enterprises toward cheaper Chinese models and self-built Claude Artifact tools.
body: Imhof's cycle centers on a cost thesis: frontier API prices are climbing and face geopolitical supply risks, while cheaper Chinese open-source models gain ground on data sovereignty and economics [1][6][12][13]. He argues the open-source to frontier gap is closing from yearly to monthly intervals, and that AI agents plus self-updating "living artifacts" threaten incumbent SaaS, suggesting Claude Artifacts as free internal SaaS and custom AI CRMs over SAP or Salesforce [3][7][8][10][11]. On strategy he advocates matching model complexity to task to maximize return, and warns US over-regulation following EU patterns may cede AI dominance to China [9][13]. Separately he tracks the Anthropic vs Microsoft agent integration fight over Teams and Slack, and Claude Tag, an internal Anthropic tool he says 60% of users interact with [0][2][4][5].
